rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Set-Aside Rulings Of July 23, 2021, March 1, 2021 And April 7, 2021 Due To Judicial Bad Faith Under Supreme Court Authority In "Broadman" Decision
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 28, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT ANNE KIHAGI'S NOTICE OF MOTION TO SET-ASIDE RULINGS OF JULY 23, 2021, MARCH 1, 2021 AND APRIL 7, 2021 DUE TO JUDICIAL BAD FAITH UNDER SUPREME COURT AUTHORITY IN "BROADMAN" DECISION is transferred to department 210 to be heard on December 1, 2021 at 11:00 a.m. per the order of the presiding judge. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SAN PABLO SR2, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. SPARX7, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 28, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT SPARX 7, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to November 17, 2021. No later than November 5, 2021 Plaintiff to submit a supplemental brief limited to the following: (1) citations to cases stating for the proposition that a non-party to a contract is bound by its terms because a party is not capable of performing the terms (See Opposition 6:1-4; TAC 6:15-19); no further argument, this section of the brief shall only include citations or state "none;" (2) facts supporting the allegation that it was the "intention and agreement of the parties that the integration clause in the Ground Lease was to only relate to the Ground Lease terms," specifically facts showing intention and facts showing agreement, e.g. how the intention was communicated, how the agreement was reached, when, by whom, etc.; no further argument, this section of the brief shall only include facts; (3) outline of all breached terms of the agreement(s) of the parties as of December 2017 after the execution of the Ground lease; this outline to be in the table format: Column One - the breached term; Column 2 - source of the term; Column 3- signatories to the document in column 2; Column 4 - specific breach upon which the first cause of action is based (TAC 6:9-7:14), e.g. for the asserted breach by Kevin Lee of "failure to sign the final Agreement to Form Joint Venture, the Construction Rider Work Letter, the Guaranty," (TAC, paragraph 23(a)) what was the term breached, in which document this term appeared and who are the signatories to that document as it appears on the document; no further argument for this part of the brief; The Court will not consider the supplemental brief if any part of it is not responsive to the request outlined in this ruling. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: GOLDEN PACIFIC PROPERTIES LLC, A CALIFORNIA VS. JEFF JOHNSTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 28, 2021 line 7. DEFENDANT JEFF JOHNSTON'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is DENIED. Moving party fails to meet the requirements for relief under any of the listed statutory basis: moving party had actual notice of the case upon service; nothing on the judgment roll indicates that the judgment is void; and no facts are presented to support surprise, mistake or excusable neglect resulting in default. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
